title: Meeko Ghintee

'''Meeko Ghintee''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Muttani]] criminal who was operating during the time of the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. A known cheat, Meeko made his dishonest living in the underworld of beast pit-fighting games, such as those held on [[Outland Transit Station]].

==Biography==
The [[Muttani]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Meeko Ghintee was [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the<ref name="BH" /> astronomical object<ref name="Muttani">{{CSWECite|II|346|Muttanni}}</ref> [[Kalidorn]].<ref name="BH" /> During the latter [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], Ghintee, a criminal nearly from birth, left street-bound petty theft and became one of the most wanted outlaws<ref name="FF125" /> in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Guide">[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> He acquired a rap sheet full of many crimes, including murder, grand larceny, and violent escapes from multiple [[Prison/Legends|prisons]]. Ghintee moved from place to place in an attempt to avoid the law, eventually settling down on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] trading [[Space station/Legends|station]] [[Outland Transit Station]] and taking over much of the crime there. He set up fixed beast fighting games, controlling beasts' behavior via remote control.<ref name="FF125">{{FactFile|125|GHI 1-2|Meeko Ghintee}}</ref> Ghintee ended up frequently incarcerated for such nefarious deeds and underhanded tactics but always made it back to the fighting arenas.<ref name="Guide" />

[[File:Miko_concept.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Meeko in [[Desolation Alley]]]]
Following the Invasion of Naboo, Meeko was wanted by the [[Republic Correctional Authority/Legends|Republic Correctional Authority]], for a number of charges including murder, assault and larceny, and became one of the highest-priority criminals of the time. Ghintee was captured and given a life sentence at [[Desolation Alley|the prison facility known as Desolation Alley]] on [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]], but he escaped, obtaining the [[Longshot (Tri-Mark VII Interceptor)|''Longshot'']] in the process. However, Meeko was not as free as he would have liked, since [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] tracked the criminal to Outland Station. Meeko initially evaded the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] by pitting his remote-controlled [[borhek]] beast against Fett, and fled into the streets. Meeko commandeered a speeder, but crashed it and fled on foot. Eventually, he was pursued to the docking bay by Jango Fett, where he attempted to finally rid himself of the bounty hunter by using his ship's onboard weapon systems against him. Fett proved too skilled, however, and severely damaged the ''Longshot'' before finally apprehending Meeko and promptly collecting his reward for the alien rogue.<ref name="BH" />

Meeko was sent back to Desolation Alley, with a second life sentence added to his existing one. However, when Jango Fett arrived on Oovo IV while tracking down [[Komari Vosa]], Meeko saw his chance to escape. Ghintee signed on to a work crew mining ore on the planetoid's rocky surface, and managed to flee the prison—and Fett—in a mining vehicle, only for Fett himself to stumble upon them while seeking his own way out. Scared witless, Meeko attempted to shoot Fett down with a rotary blaster cannon, but the bounty hunter once again proved too skilled for the criminal, and swiftly killed Meeko. Ironically, Meeko had a considerable bounty on his head at the time (10,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|Republic credits]]), which Fett promptly collected. Even more ironically, the bounty was posted by the owner of the [[borhek]] which was stolen by Meeko and used to cheat in an Outland Station championship beast pit fight.<ref name="BH" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the video game ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', players can mark Meeko as a bounty at [[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter #Merchant Row|Merchant Row]] midway through chapter one, but they cannot secure the bounty until the cut-scene at the end of chapter one.

Later in the game, on the level "[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter#Chapter 3|Chapter 3: The Break Out]]," Meeko is a secondary bounty who can be killed for 10,000 credits, the highest secondary bounty in the game. He is found in a cave near the end of the level, along with three other prisoners. They are trying to work a mining machine in order to break out of Desolation Alley prison on Oovo IV; he is also the last secondary bounty of the level. The strategy guide incorrectly lists his homeworld as "Kaldun."
